LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S PETITION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION & REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. THE PARTIES ARE REQUIRED TO ARBITRATE THEIR DISPUTES, BUT WILL NEED TO SELECT A NEW PANEL OF ARBITRATORS. THE INTERESTS OF JUSTICE STRONGLY SUPPORT THE PARTIES SELECTING A NEW ARBITRATION PANEL. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hrg On Petition For An Order Compelling Arb & Req For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-27-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert Stanley Venning, Esq., Tel. 510.339.8025, appearing for petitioner and moving party.
Brian Kelly, Esq., of Duane Morris, Tel. 415.957.3213, appearing for respondent and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S Petition For An Order Compelling Arbitration & Request For Sanctions is Granted in part. The parties are required to arbitrate their disputes, but will need to select a new panel of arbitrators. The interests of justice strongly support the parties selecting a new arbitration panel. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

Ntc Of Hrg On Petition For An Order Compelling Arb & Req For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-21-2012 Continued To Jun-27-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert Stanley Venning, Esq., 510-339-8025, for Petitioner and Moving party Pacific Gas and Electric Company
Brian A. Kelly, Esq., 415-957-3213, for Commercial Union Insurance Company

Disclosure made by Judge Kahn, that more that 20 years ago, he worked in same law firm where Mr. Venning was a partner, and that he doesn't think he had any contact with Mr. Venning in the last 25 years.

Argued. The court vacated its tentative ruling. The parties agreed to arbitrate.
PETITIONER PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition For An Order Compelling Arbitration & Request For Sanctions is continued to June 27, 2012 at 9:30 a.m., to discuss who is going to be the arbitrator, further opposition due June 6, supplemental reply due June 21, courtesy copies of both by 4 p.m. on June 21. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

RULING:
A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PETITIONER PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S Motion For Order Requiring Respondent To Comply With Court's Prior Order Compelling Arbitration: GRANTED; the Court orders Counsel to exchange the list of potential arbitrators on or before January 8, 2004.
